_doc: Source-of-truth benchmarks for tests/parity_test.py. Do not embed these values in the test file — read from here so the spec is reviewable and changeable without touching test code.

source.dataset: Jon-Becker/prediction-market-analysis — 5M-row stratified sample of 67M-row resolved-trades materialization (2023-08 through 2025-11)

_tolerance_doc: User-specified ±0.10 percentage points per Step 3 directive 2026-05-14. Research docs originally cited 0.05 pp; widened tolerance accommodates the 0.02-0.05 pp drift documented in DataPipeline.md §1.4 between Becker (2026) and this pipeline.

maker_excess_return_pct.description: Average maker-side excess return across all resolved trades. Becker's headline figure.

maker_no_excess_return_pct.description: Maker excess return on trades where the taker took the YES side (so the maker is on NO). Higher than YES-side; constitutes the Optimism Tax.

maker_yes_excess_return_pct.description: Maker excess return on trades where the taker took the NO side (so the maker is on YES). Lower than NO-side; underside of the Optimism Tax.

directional_invariant_no_gt_yes.description: Optimism Tax — maker NO excess must exceed maker YES excess. Asymmetric retail pricing of cheap YES outcomes.
directional_invariant_no_gt_yes._gap_doc: Becker reference gap is 1.25 - 0.77 = 0.48 pp. Min threshold 0.30 pp gives ~40% headroom for pipeline drift while still detecting a structural sign flip.

unverified_assertions.name: yes_taker_volume_share_1_to_10c_bin
unverified_assertions.description: User-specified Step 3 assertion: YES taker volume at 1-10¢ price bin is 40-48% of total taker volume in that bin.
unverified_assertions.search_log: grep across /root/research/EdgePulse_Backtest_*.md, EdgePulse_Engineering_*.md, EdgePulse_Operating_*.md, and /root/research/wealth_transfer_drafts/ returned no citation for this specific 40-48% range. No becker_benchmarks.json predated this file. No *.ipynb on the server. Closest documented Optimism-Tax claim is the maker NO vs YES asymmetry (covered above).
unverified_assertions.to_enable: Provide a primary source (Becker paper section, repo notebook cell, or direct measurement against the transformed parquet) for the 40-48% range. Add fields `min_pct` and `max_pct` here, then remove the @pytest.mark.skip on test_assertion_3_yes_taker_share_1_to_10c_bin in parity_test.py.

_per_strategy_doc: Engineering S.4 per-strategy backtest parity expectations. Each verdict strategy with verdict-doc-reported annual P&L, trade count, mean return, win rate. tests/test_per_strategy_parity.py iterates these and asserts production scanner output matches within 10% P&L / 1% signal-count tolerance per Engineering S.4. Source: /root/research/EdgePulse_Backtest_Verdict_2026.md §3-4 per-strategy tables.
